meta.title: biblio

source.publication: Section 4: Roots, Prefixes, Suffixes and Foreign Words (owner-supplied reference)
source.usage_note: Personal study only. Root, words and meanings transcribed faithfully from the source PDF; a small number of well-attested real words on the same root are included where the source list was too short to form a complete family (4 to 8 members). Parts, glosses, context sentences, mentor note, attempt and construct options are the teaching layer, authored for the Language Garden (LANGUAGE_GARDEN_BIBLE §10).

meta.reviewer_notes: Batch 2: transcribed from the owner's roots (Greek and Latin) reference; 2 taught, 2 reach.

root.label: biblio
root.mentor_note: biblio is the root behind almost every word English has for the love, sale, or study of books.

attempt.direction_prompt: Indifferent to books, or collects them obsessively?

context_sentences: As a lifelong bibliophile, she judged any new city by the quality of its secondhand bookshops.
The estate sale drew bibliophiles from three counties, all hoping for a first edition.
The thesis’s bibliography ran to eleven pages, longer than some of its actual chapters.
An examiner can often tell how carefully a student worked just from the bibliography alone.
Friends gently called it bibliomania, though his flat, stacked floor to ceiling, suggested something more serious.
The auction house’s rare books department exists entirely because of collectors with a touch of bibliomania.

construct_options.text: An extreme, obsessive passion for collecting books
A serious illness caused by reading in poor light
An extreme dislike of reading or owning books

context_sentences: The old bibliopole knew the value of every worn spine on his shelves without checking a single catalogue.
Before the internet, a good bibliopole was often the only way to track down an out of print title.

construct_options.text: A person who sells books, especially rare or old ones
A person who writes reviews of newly published books
A person who repairs and restores damaged books
